text.title: ES Aggregate Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to introduce a new event-sourced aggregate.

step.name: list-events-and-commands
step.action: Enumerate past-tense events (`OrderPlaced`, `ItemAdded`, ...) and command methods (`place`, `add_item`, ...). Map each command to the events it can emit.

step.name: write-aggregate
step.action: Implement the aggregate: command methods validate + append to `self._pending`; for each event, write `_apply_<EventName>(self, event)` that mutates state. No direct field assignment in commands.
step.decision-gate: If any command mutates state outside an apply handler, FAIL.

step.name: write-from-events
step.action: Add `from_events(stream_id, events)` class method that constructs an empty aggregate and replays through apply handlers. Property-based test: live execution and replay produce equal state.

step.name: repository-with-version
step.action: Implement `load(stream_id) -> (Aggregate, version)` and `save(stream_id, events, expected_version) -> new_version`. Save calls `aggregate.collect_pending_events()` AFTER successful append.

step.name: concurrency-test
step.action: Test: two transactions load the same stream, both append events, second save raises `ConcurrencyError`. Assert no events appended for the loser.
step.decision-gate: If the test passes without raising, expected_version is not enforced; fix before merging.
